rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly says it extends reference-price work and studies price perceptions (perceived savings/distance) with a moderator tied to distrust of advertised original price, while the cited paper is a core reference-price paper on how confidence shifts internal reference prices. Given this strong construct overlap and a verified DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.44.3.401), this is plausibly a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than an obvious incidental mention.
